message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): add skill-catalog callout in §1.2 with all three jeffreys-skills.md URLs

Integrate the canonical URLs for the three skills the article is about,
so readers can open each skill's catalog page directly without hunting
around for them, and so the article explicitly surfaces which skill
pairs with which phase of the pipeline.

Placement: directly under the §1.2 "Install the two skills" subheading,
before the three install paths (jsm / plugins / symlink) — this is where
the reader's attention first lands on "where do I get these", and a
skill-page link + the jsm install command share a mental model (same
slug, same account, same subscription).

Links added, verbatim from the canonical catalog:
  - /skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-1-extraction
  - /skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-2-setup-and-import
  - /skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-3-ongoing-mattermost-maintainance

Each link is annotated with its role in the pipeline (Slack-side extract,
Mattermost-side deploy/import, ongoing maintenance) so even a user
staring at the URLs alone can infer what maps to what.

An explicit login caveat is included below the list: the URLs 404
unless the reader is signed in to a jeffreys-skills.md account with an
active subscription. Ties the catalog page to the same authentication
the `jsm` CLI uses, so a reader who resolves the 404 first has already
passed the auth step `jsm install` would otherwise trigger.

Design notes:
  - Uses the same gradient-tinted card vocabulary already established
    for the download CTA and insight cards in this article (purple →
    cyan → emerald, cyan-tinted border, backdrop-blur).
  - target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" on each outbound link.
  - Full URL rendered as visible text (monospaced, break-all) so it's
    legible even if copy-pasted out of context, and so the slug
    telegraphs which skill you're about to visit.
  - No new CSS; all styling is Tailwind utilities already used in
    neighbouring sections.

Verified:
  - eslint clean on slack-migration-article.tsx
  - bun run build succeeds; /writing/slack-mattermost-migration still
    prerendered as static (○ in the build manifest).

files.patch:
@@ -1006,6 +1006,51 @@ Get-Content $HOME\\.ssh\\id_ed25519.pub`} Install the two skills + +
+

+ Skill catalog pages · open in a new tab +

+
    +
  • + + jeffreys-skills.md/skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-1-extraction + + — Phase 1, the Slack-side extraction skill. +
    • +
  • + + jeffreys-skills.md/skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-2-setup-and-import + + — Phase 2, the Mattermost-side deploy + import skill. +
    • +
  • + + jeffreys-skills.md/skills/slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-3-ongoing-mattermost-maintainance + + — Phase 3, the ongoing-maintenance skill (weekly sweep, auto-rollback upgrades, restore drills). +
    • +
+

+ Heads up: those three URLs return a 404 unless you are signed in to a jeffreys-skills.md account with an active subscription. If you hit a 404, sign up (or log in) at jeffreys-skills.md/dashboard first, then reload — the same account is what the jsm CLI authenticates against below, so doing this step first means jsm install will already know who you are. +

+
+

{"Three ways to install. Pick whichever matches the path you chose above."}
